shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,W,L  -> value_profile AND value_profile_cmplog RESOLVE; cmplog AND naive BLOCK. vp-PRO family: CMP_MAP distance gradient resolves where I2S substitution + edge-only feedback cannot.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (vp,vpc resolve / cmp,naive block). This evidence test is the INDEPENDENT verification (G2): it measures what each arm's CORPUS reaches (operand-distance for the one scalar gate; parse DEPTH for the structural/grammar gates), never the resolve label.
round0_postmortem: Round-0 bet BOTH hypotheses on value_distance_reached over a scalar 'gradient_cmp_target' operand. Across 18 branches it was UNMEASURABLE on 12 (no extractable scalar operand on the libxml2/sqlite3 structural & derived-state gates) and rule_not_met on the rest. The miss: most of this shape is NOT a fixed-offset scalar literal-distance gate. The gate_structure slots make this explicit -- 12/18 are presence_or_nonnull / derived_integer / state_or_grammar, where the 'target' is a deeper PARSE STATE (a two-dot qualified name, a default-namespace node with prefix==NULL, a VACUUM/SELECT-with-operator parse), not a byte literal at a known offset. value_distance_reached cannot find a target there because there is no scalar to land on. VP's advantage on those branches is STRUCTURAL DEPTH (its corpus reaches deeper into the gating call-chain), measurable with depth_reach -- a built tool round-0 never used.

hypotheses.label: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient lands its corpus closer to a FIXED scalar gate operand than cmplog/naive get -- the genuine value-distance subtype, restricted to the one gate that has an extractable contiguous literal AND on-disk seeds

params.width_caveat: operand width 2 (<<) -> short-operand chance-alignment flag per tool scope_limits; this is the tool's own validated anchor (vpc reaches '<<' dist 0 vs cmp/naive 0.5), so accept the anchor confidence.

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if VP's gradient drives the corpus onto the '<<' shift operator that gates OP_ShiftLeft, the vp/vpc resolving seeds land ON or NEAR '<<' (winner_min ~ 0) while the cmplog/naive blocking seeds stall away from it (distance_gap > 0, winner_closer true). ALTERNATIVES ruled out: (a) plain coverage luck predicts distance_gap ~ 0 (VP got no closer, it resolved by corpus volume); (b) an I2S-substitution story predicts cmplog ALSO closes the distance (cmp_min_distance ~ 0) since '<<' would be lifted from a logged CMP -- cmplog NOT closing discriminates gradient from I2S, consistent with cmplog being a Layer-1 LOSER here.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 AND cmp_min_distance >= 0.40

hypotheses.label: value_profile's gradient-built corpus reaches a DEEPER parse/codegen state along the gating call-chain than cmplog/naive -- the structural/derived/grammar subtype where the gate is a parse DEPTH, not a scalar operand

params.rationale: These 6 branches are derived_integer / state_or_grammar gates (cntTab increments, zDb!=0 two-dot name, operator-expression codegen, sqlite3TwoPartName, VACUUM reduce, LALR yy_reduce). The 'target' is a parse STATE reached by assembling a deeper token structure, not a byte literal -- exactly the branches value_distance_reached returned UNMEASURABLE on. depth_reach runs each arm's saved seeds through the libafl-sqlite3-cov image and reads how deep the branch line executes + whether the blocked side is taken, per-seed-normalized. Measures what the corpus REACHED, independent of the resolve label (G2).

hypotheses.prediction: G1: if VP's gradient climbs the parse chain, value_profile's resolving corpus drives the blocker line far deeper / takes the blocked side, while naive's blocking corpus stalls shallow (depth_lift >> 1, winner_deeper true, side_lift > 1). The ALTERNATIVE this rules out: 'no real gradient -- VP resolved by a single lucky jump' predicts the two corpora reach EQUAL intermediate parse depth (depth_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper false) and only the final blocker label differs. A discriminating, falsifiable split: if depth_lift comes back ~1 the structural-climb story is refuted for that branch and it goes to fallback.
hypotheses.rule: winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.5 AND side_lift >= 1.5

undecidable.reason: data_realities: on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3. lcms and libxml2 have NO on-disk seed corpus, so EVERY seed-reading tool (value_distance_reached, depth_reach -- which runs saved seeds through the cov image, joint_necessity) returns no input on these 11 branches. This is a genuine data-coverage gap, not a missing measurement: there is no built (and no proposable, given the corpora simply don't exist) descriptor that can score VP's advantage here. Several are ALSO mechanistically unmeasurable even with corpus: lcms_2143 is a fixed-offset FOURCC ('prtr') -- value-distance-shaped but corpus-absent; libxml2 6926/6948/7317 are presence_or_nonnull structural gates with no scalar AND no corpus. Honest decidable:false rather than re-propose an operand the data cannot supply (G3).
undecidable.note: lcms_2143 is the cleanest scalar-distance INSTANCE in the whole shape (fixed-offset 'prtr' FOURCC, offset 0x0c) and WOULD be a vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance member if lcms had an on-disk corpus -- it does not, so it falls here. If an lcms corpus is later captured, move it to vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance with params operand_literal='prtr', mode=fourcc4.

priors_dropped: vp_gradient_value_distance_closed (round-0 H1) -- DROPPED as the shape-wide hypothesis; survives ONLY as vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance restricted to the single branch (sqlite3_14437) that has both an extractable contiguous operand and on-disk seeds. The round-0 framing applied it to 18 branches; 17 have no scalar operand and/or no corpus.

vp_gradient_corpus_reaches_deeper (round-0 H2) -- the depth idea was right but it was authored as a NOT-YET-BUILT coverage_timeseries:depth_reach descriptor with a difference-based rule (depth_lift >= 0.20). REPLACED by the BUILT seed_set:depth_reach tool whose depth_lift is a RATIO (winner/loser line count), so the rule is now ratio-thresholded (>= 1.5) over the tool's real metrics.
data_realities_respected: on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 -> only the 6 sqlite3 branches are scorable; the 1 lcms + 10 libxml2 branches are decidable:false (no seeds for any tool to read).

I2S NOT tagged in mutation_op / VP is a FEEDBACK not a mutator -> neither test reads a 'gradient event'. value_distance_reached reads only the saved CORPUS operand bytes; depth_reach reads only what the corpus's coverage reaches. VP's effect is inferred from what the corpus REACHED, never from a lineage/mutation event.

structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds -> byte-offset Hamming alignment fails on them; those 6 sqlite3 branches use depth_reach (coverage of the gate line), NOT fixed-offset value-distance. Only the single contiguous-operand branch with a short fixed token (sqlite3_14437 '<<') uses value_distance_reached, and the tool's own sliding-window min-Hamming handles its size variance.

both tests are INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): value-distance measures operand proximity in the corpus, depth_reach measures parse-line execution depth in the corpus -- neither reads whether vp/vpc resolved the blocker.
notes: COLLAPSE: round-0's 5 source-semantic subtypes (literal_token_assembly, multistage_parse_path, single_byte_boundary, namespace_structure_path, derived_structural_state) re-partitioned into TWO MEASURABLE mechanisms + one honest decidable:false. (1) vp_gradient_scalar_operand_distance -- the genuine fixed-operand value-distance subtype, but only ONE branch (sqlite3_14437) satisfies BOTH 'has an extractable contiguous literal' AND 'has on-disk seeds'. (2) vp_gradient_structural_depth -- the 6-branch sqlite3 derived/grammar subtype the round-0 value-distance descriptor was UNMEASURABLE on; their VP advantage is reaching a deeper parse state, measured by the BUILT depth_reach tool with no scalar operand needed. NEW MECHANISM PROPOSED: structural-depth-reach as the discriminator for VP-pro on derived/grammar gates -- distinct from value-distance because there is no target byte; the observable is corpus parse-depth, and the discriminating alternative ('lucky jump, no climb') predicts depth_lift~1. NO NEW DESCRIPTOR built: both hypotheses reuse a registry tool (value_distance_reached, depth_reach). DROPPED both round-0 hypotheses as shape-wide (see priors_dropped). DECIDABLE:FALSE sub-group = lcms + all libxml2 (11 branches) -- no on-disk corpus for any seed-reading tool; this is the correct G3 outcome, not a re-proposed unmeasurable operand. NOTE on 14437: it anchors the scalar hypothesis (value_distance_reached's validated anchor); decision_order tries the scalar test FIRST and only falls through to structural-depth if scalar is rule_not_met, so no double-count. If the arbiter prefers corroboration, 14437 may be added as a 7th member of vp_gradient_structural_depth -- left scalar-first by decision_order to keep the cleaner anchor.
